---
title: "What Makes an App Successful?"
author: "Annika Rodriguez"
date: "2026-05-01"
output:
  pdf_document: default
  html_document: default
---

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(caret)
library(randomForest)
library(pROC)
library(corrplot)

apps <- read.csv("~/Documents/MSDA/Spring 2026/Data Analytics Application/Final Project/Google Play Store/archive (5)/googleplaystore.csv")
reviews <- read.csv("~/Documents/MSDA/Spring 2026/Data Analytics Application/Final Project/Google Play Store/archive (5)/googleplaystore_user_reviews.csv")
```


```{r}
apps_clean <- apps |>
  filter(!is.na(Rating)) |>
  distinct(App, .keep_all = TRUE) |>
  mutate(
    Reviews = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(Reviews)),
    Installs = gsub("[+,]", "", Installs),
    Installs = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(Installs)),
    
    Price = gsub("[$]", "", Price),
    Price = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(Price)),
    
    Type = as.factor(Type),
    Category = as.factor(Category),
    Content.Rating = as.factor(Content.Rating),

    High_Performance = ifelse(Installs >= 1000000, "Yes", "No"),
    High_Performance = as.factor(High_Performance)) |>
  filter(!is.na(Reviews), !is.na(Installs), !is.na(Price), !is.na(Type),
         Category != "1.9")

str(apps_clean)
summary(apps_clean)
dim(apps_clean)
```


```{r}
reviews_clean <- reviews |> 
  filter(!is.na(Sentiment_Polarity), !is.na(Sentiment_Subjectivity)) |> 
  group_by(App) |> 
  summarize(
    Avg_Sentiment_Polarity = mean(Sentiment_Polarity, na.rm = TRUE),
    Avg_Sentiment_Subjectivity = mean(Sentiment_Subjectivity, na.rm = TRUE),
    Positive_Reviews = sum(Sentiment == "Positive", na.rm = TRUE),
    Negative_Reviews = sum(Sentiment == "Negative", na.rm = TRUE),
    Neutral_Reviews = sum(Sentiment == "Neutral", na.rm = TRUE))

str(reviews_clean)
summary(reviews_clean)
dim(reviews_clean)
```


```{r}
#Merge datasets
final_data <- apps_clean |> 
  left_join(reviews_clean, by = "App") |> 
  mutate(
    Avg_Sentiment_Polarity = ifelse(is.na(Avg_Sentiment_Polarity), 0, Avg_Sentiment_Polarity),
    Avg_Sentiment_Subjectivity = ifelse(is.na(Avg_Sentiment_Subjectivity), 0, Avg_Sentiment_Subjectivity),
    Positive_Reviews = ifelse(is.na(Positive_Reviews), 0, Positive_Reviews),
    Negative_Reviews = ifelse(is.na(Negative_Reviews), 0, Negative_Reviews),
    Neutral_Reviews = ifelse(is.na(Neutral_Reviews), 0, Neutral_Reviews))

final_data <- final_data |>
  filter(Category != "1.9")
final_data$Category <- droplevels(final_data$Category)

str(final_data)
summary(final_data)
dim(final_data)
```


EDA CHARTS

```{r}
#distribution of app ratings 
ggplot(final_data, aes(x = Rating)) +
  geom_histogram(
    bins = 30,
    fill = "#4285F4",
    color = "black") +
  labs(title = "Distribution of App Ratings",
       x = "App Rating",
       y = "Number of Applications") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold"),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 12))
```



```{r}
#Free vs Paid ratings
ggplot(final_data, aes(x = Type, y = Rating, fill = Type)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(title = "Ratings by App Type",
       x = "App Type",
       y = "Rating") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold"),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 12))
```

```{r}
#reviews vs installs
ggplot(final_data, aes(x = Reviews, y = Installs)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.4) +
  scale_x_log10() +
  scale_y_log10() +
  labs(title = "Relationship Between Reviews and Installs",
       x = "Reviews (Log Scale)",
       y = "Installs (Log Scale)") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold"),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 12))
```

```{r}
#Distribution of User Review Sentiment
reviews |>
  filter(!is.na(Sentiment),
         Sentiment != "nan") |>
  ggplot(aes(x = Sentiment, fill = Sentiment)) +
  geom_bar() +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(
    "Positive" = "lightgreen",
    "Neutral" = "gold",
    "Negative" = "tomato")) +
  labs(title = "Distribution of User Review Sentiment",
       x = "Sentiment",
       y = "Number of Reviews") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold"),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 12))
```

```{r}
#top categories by average rating
final_data |> 
  group_by(Category) |> 
  summarise(Avg_Rating = mean(Rating, na.rm = TRUE)) |> 
  arrange(desc(Avg_Rating)) |> 
  head(10) |> 
  ggplot(aes(x = reorder(Category, Avg_Rating), y = Avg_Rating)) +
  geom_col(fill = "lightpink") +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(title = "Top 10 Categories by Average Rating",
       x = "Category",
       y = "Average Rating") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold"),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 12))
```

```{r}
# correlation heatmap
numeric_data <- final_data |>
  select(
    Rating,
    Reviews,
    Installs,
    Price,
    Avg_Sentiment_Polarity,
    Avg_Sentiment_Subjectivity,
    Positive_Reviews,
    Negative_Reviews,
    Neutral_Reviews)

cor_matrix <- cor(numeric_data)

corrplot( cor_matrix, method = "color", type = "upper", tl.cex = 0.8, number.cex = 0.7)
```



Hypothesis Tests

```{r}
# Ttest Do free and paid apps have different ratings?
t.test(Rating ~ Type, data = final_data)
```

```{r}
# correlation Are reviews related to ratings?
cor.test(final_data$Reviews, final_data$Rating)
```

```{r}
#ANOVA Do ratings differ by category?
anova_model <- aov(Rating ~ Category, data = final_data)
summary(anova_model)
```


Modeling

```{r}
model_data <- final_data |> 
  select(High_Performance, Rating, Reviews, Price, Type,
         Avg_Sentiment_Polarity, Avg_Sentiment_Subjectivity) |> 
  na.omit()

set.seed(123)
train_index <- createDataPartition(model_data$High_Performance, p = 0.8, list = FALSE)

train_data <- model_data[train_index, ]
test_data <- model_data[-train_index, ]
```

```{r}
#logistic Regression
log_model <- glm(High_Performance ~ ., data = train_data, family = "binomial")

log_probs <- predict(log_model, test_data, type = "response")
log_preds <- ifelse(log_probs > 0.5, "Yes", "No")
log_preds <- as.factor(log_preds)

confusionMatrix(log_preds, test_data$High_Performance, positive = "Yes")
```

```{r}
#Random Forest
set.seed(123)

rf_model <- randomForest(High_Performance ~ ., data = train_data, ntree = 500, importance = TRUE)

rf_preds <- predict(rf_model, test_data)

confusionMatrix(rf_preds, test_data$High_Performance, positive = "Yes")
```

```{r}
#variable importance
varImpPlot(rf_model)
```

```{r}
#ROC curve for Random forest
rf_probs <- predict(rf_model, test_data, type = "prob")[, "Yes"]

roc_rf <- roc(test_data$High_Performance, rf_probs)

plot(roc_rf,
     main = "ROC Curve - Random Forest",
     col = "red",
     lwd = 3)

auc(roc_rf)
```
